title: INHERIT v1 Root Schema
description: Root entry point for an INHERIT v1 estate data interchange document. Contains a single estate and arrays of all entity types
$comment: $ref resolution convention: all $ref paths are resolved relative to the schema's $id URI, NOT filesystem paths. Core schemas use relative paths (e.g., 'common/money.json' resolves to 'https://openinherit.org/v1/common/money.json'). Extension schemas use '../common/money.json' (resolves relative to their $id in the extensions/ directory). All schemas MUST be registered with the validator before $ref resolution will work.

@context.description: JSON-LD context URI. When present, this document can be processed by any JSON-LD processor as linked data. Maps INHERIT fields to Schema.org, FIBO, Wikidata, and GS1 vocabularies

inherit.description: Schema identifier — must always be 'https://openinherit.org/v1/schema.json'. Used by consumers to identify this as an INHERIT v1 document

version.description: Major version number of the INHERIT schema. Always 1 for v1 documents

schemaVersion.description: Semver version of the INHERIT schema this document was created against. Enables consumers to detect compatibility and handle graceful degradation

exportedAt.description: Date this document was exported from the producing system

exportedBy.description: The person who exported or prepared this document

name.description: Name of the person who exported the document

email.description: Email address of the person who exported the document

generator.description: The software that generated this INHERIT document

url.description: URL of the generating software or organisation

estate.description: The single estate record for this document

people.description: All people involved in the estate — testator, beneficiaries, executors, witnesses, guardians, etc

kinships.description: Familial bonds between people in the estate. Essential for intestacy calculations

relationships.description: Spousal and partnership relationships. Determines marital property rights and surviving spouse claims

properties.description: Real estate owned or partly owned by the testator

assets.description: Non-property assets — financial accounts, personal property, vehicles, digital assets, etc

assetCollections.description: Named groups of related assets — collections (model railways, art, wine, etc.) that have collective significance and value

assetInterests.description: Informal beneficiary interest in specific assets or collections, captured before formal bequest allocation

liabilities.description: Debts and financial obligations of the estate, including cultural obligations (mahr, ketubah debt)

bequests.description: Dispositions of property or money in the will

trusts.description: Trusts — testamentary or inter vivos — associated with the estate

executors.description: Persons appointed to administer the estate

wishes.description: Non-binding (or culturally binding) wishes — funeral, letters, pet care, digital estate

documents.description: Documents associated with the estate — wills, codicils, trust deeds, certificates

nonprobateTransfers.description: Assets that pass outside the probate estate — JTWROS, TOD deeds, beneficiary designations

proxyAuthorisations.description: Authorisations for proxies to act on behalf of the testator

valuations.description: Valuations of assets, properties, and collections. Multiple valuations per entity are supported — owner estimates, professional valuations, AI estimates, dealer offers

lifetimeTransfers.description: Gifts and transfers made during the testator's lifetime. Critical for inheritance tax calculations — UK 7-year rule, US unified gift/estate tax, etc

dealerInterests.description: Third-party interest in estate assets — dealers, investors, collectors

dataProvenance.description: Default data provenance for all entities in this document. Individual entities may override this with their own provenance. Tracks how the data was originally captured
dataProvenance.$comment: manual_entry: data entered by hand by a person. ai_extracted: extracted by an AI model (e.g. INHERIT Scanner). ocr_scanned: extracted via optical character recognition. imported: imported from another system (Clio, LEAP, etc.). migrated: migrated from a legacy format or previous version. system_generated: generated by software without human input.

importSources.description: Systems from which data was imported into this document. Enables audit trail and source tracking

id.description: Unique identifier for this import source, referenced by importSourceId on entities

importDate.description: Date the data was imported from this source

importMethod.description: How the data was imported from this source
importMethod.$comment: api: imported via API integration. file_upload: imported from an uploaded file (CSV, JSON, etc.). manual_copy: manually copied from the source. ai_extraction: extracted by an AI model from documents or images. ocr: extracted via optical character recognition from scanned documents. migration_script: automated migration from a legacy system.

examples: Migrated from Clio matter #12345 — assets and liabilities only

completeness.description: Estate completeness score — jurisdiction-aware checklist tracking what data has been captured, what's missing, and how complete the estate plan is

taxPosition.description: Estimated tax position summary computed from valuations, lifetime transfers, exemptions, and jurisdiction-specific thresholds. Always includes a disclaimer — this is informational, not tax advice

recommendedActions.description: Recommended next actions generated from data gaps, urgency flags, incomplete transfer history, missing valuations, and estate status. Actions adapt to the current lifecycle stage

category.$comment: completeness: filling data gaps. tax_planning: optimizing tax position. legal_requirement: meeting statutory obligations. valuation: getting or updating valuations. transfer_history: documenting lifetime gifts. beneficiary_review: reviewing nominations and designations. document_update: updating legal documents.

priority.$comment: critical: must address immediately — legal deadline or compliance risk. high: should address soon — significant financial or legal impact. medium: address when convenient — improves estate plan quality. low: nice to have — marginal improvement.

examples: Get professional valuation for share portfolio
Document lifetime gifts in lookback period

description.description: Detailed explanation of what to do and why

status.$comment: pending: not yet started. in_progress: being worked on. completed: done. dismissed: user chose not to act on this.

triggeredBy.description: What data gap or condition generated this action
examples: Asset 'Barclays shares' has estimatedValue > £50,000 but no professional valuation

relatedEntityType.description: Entity type this action relates to

relatedEntityId.description: ID of the specific entity this action relates to

conformance.description: Machine-readable conformance certificate — records the validation level, schema version, completeness score, and provenance summary at time of validation

level.$comment: level_1: structural validity — document parses and validates against JSON Schema. level_2: referential integrity — all cross-references resolve. level_3: jurisdiction complete — all required fields for the estate's jurisdiction are present.

validatedBy.description: Name of the tool that performed the validation

schemaVersion.description: INHERIT schema version this document was validated against

completenessScore.description: Completeness score at time of validation

entityCounts.description: Count of entities per type at time of validation

provenanceSummary.description: Count of entities by data provenance source

warnings.description: Validation warnings — non-blocking issues that may need attention
examples: 2 assets have estimated but no professional valuation
Lifetime transfer history covers only 5 of 7 required lookback years

legacyContacts.description: People nominated to receive access to the estate data or catalogue when the account owner dies or becomes incapacitated. This is a digital inheritance concept, not a legal role — legacy contacts are notified and given access, not granted legal authority. The 'Please open when I have passed away' letter is generated by the application, not stored here

id.description: Unique identifier for this legacy contact

personId.description: Reference to a Person.id, if the legacy contact is also in the people array

name.description: Display name — required because catalogue-only documents may not have a people array

notificationMethod.$comment: email: send notification by email. phone: call or text. post: physical letter. in_person: notify face to face.

accessLevel.description: What level of access this person should receive
accessLevel.$comment: full: complete access to all data. read_only: can view but not modify. collection_only: can only see asset collections and items. financial_only: can only see financial information.

letterGenerated.description: Whether the 'Please open when I have passed away' letter has been generated for this contact

letterGeneratedAt.description: When the letter was last generated

letterDeliveryMethod.description: How the letter was or should be delivered

notes.description: Additional notes about this legacy contact

extensions.description: URIs of extension schemas applied to this document. Consumers should load and validate against these extensions
extensions.$comment: URIs of extension schemas applied to this document.

^x-inherit-.$comment: Extension data blocks. Each can contain any structure incl. arrays. Extension schema validates.
